DDD Intervention Districts Usage Insights

Data Driven Districts · New Leaders Foundation

Data as at 23 March 2026
District status
On trackSchool-level accounts ≥50% active, underperforming schools reached ≥50%, and non-school level ≥50% active
Usage support requiredSchool-level accounts ≥25% active or underperforming schools reached ≥25%, but does not meet all three On track thresholds
Low usage, targeted support requiredSchool-level accounts <25% active and underperforming schools reached <25%
Circular D3 of 2023 — severity tiers
Primary schools (Grade 6)
A school is flagged as underperforming when <60% of its Grade 6 learners achieve Level 4 or above in EITHER the Language of Learning and Teaching (LOLT) OR Mathematics.
Critical (<30%)Either subject has <30% at Level 4+. Emergency intervention needed.
Severe (<45%)Either subject <45% (but both ≥30%). Sustained multi-term support.
Moderate (<60%)Below 60% but ≥45% in both. Closest to D3 threshold.
Secondary schools (NSC)
A school is flagged as underperforming when (A) its NSC pass rate falls below 70%, OR (B) it has met the 70% pass rate but produced less than 30% bachelor passes. Both the pass rate AND quality of passes (bachelor) matter.
Critical (<50%)NSC pass rate below 50%. Emergency intervention needed.
Severe (<70%)NSC pass rate below 70% (but ≥50%). Sustained multi-term support.
Moderate (<30% BP)Met 70% pass rate but bachelor passes below 30%. Quality concern.

Intervention districts

Data Driven Districts Programme · New Leaders Foundation
Primary D3: EITHER LOLT or Maths <60% at Level 4+ (participation-based LOLT) | Secondary D3: NSC pass rate <70% or bachelor passes <30%
